title: 360° Orbit Around a Standoff
prompt: A smooth 360-degree orbital shot circles two figures facing each other in [LOCATION: an empty intersection] at dusk. The camera maintains constant height and distance, revealing the surrounding empty city as it rotates. Long shadows, amber sky fading to blue, streetlights flickering on mid-orbit. 40mm lens, gimbal-smooth motion, no cuts. Wind and distant traffic ambience. Tense, operatic.

title: Crash Zoom Discovery
prompt: Static wide shot of a gray coastline, waves rolling under an overcast sky. A sudden crash zoom punches in on a tiny figure standing at the waterline, holding something to their chest. Slight handheld instability after the zoom settles, as if the operator just spotted them. 1970s documentary zoom feel, muted desaturated grade, wind buffeting the microphone.

title: Handheld Follow Through a Crowd
prompt: Urgent handheld camera follows close behind [SUBJECT] as they push through [LOCATION: a crowded night market], weaving between vendors and strangers. The camera stays at shoulder height, occasionally bumped, lens flaring off hanging bulbs and neon signs. 28mm wide lens, shallow focus snapping between the subject and passing faces. Dense layered crowd audio, sizzling food, overlapping voices. Kinetic, immersive, anxious handheld street-cinema energy. Single continuous take unless a lens switch is written; characters stay consistent and singular; grounded, stable physics throughout.

title: Snorricam Spiral
prompt: Snorricam rig locked to [SUBJECT]'s chest as they stumble through a night street in panic — their face pinned steady in frame while the entire world lurches and spins behind them. Streetlights smear into streaks, passersby blur past. 24mm wide, slightly low angle up at their face. Disorienting, subjective, a psychological breakdown rendered in camera grammar. Heartbeat and muffled city under ragged breathing.

title: Vertigo Dolly Zoom
prompt: Medium shot of [SUBJECT] standing at the end of a long pier as the camera performs a slow dolly zoom — physically pulling back while zooming in — so they stay the same size while the horizon and pier stretch sickeningly behind them. The world elongates as realization hits their face. The classic vertigo dolly-zoom effect, queasy and unhurried. The ambient sound thins out to a ringing tone.

title: Gutter-Level Tracking
prompt: Camera skims an inch above wet asphalt, tracking alongside [SUBJECT]'s boots as they walk [LOCATION: a rainy street] at night — puddles ahead reflecting inverted neon signs, each footstep shattering a reflection as the camera glides through the spray. 32mm at gutter level, droplets occasionally hitting the lens. Moody, tactile, the city seen from below. Footsteps, rain, distant jazz from a doorway.

title: Clean Over-the-Shoulder
prompt: Textbook over-the-shoulder coverage: framing past [SUBJECT A]'s shoulder and jawline (soft, out of focus, anchoring the left third) onto [SUBJECT B]'s face in clean focus as they listen and respond. Eyeline just off-lens. 50mm at chest height, soft key wrapping the listener's face. The standard conversation building block, shot to cut seamlessly with its reverse.

title: Insert: Hands Tell It
prompt: Insert shot coverage: [SUBJECT]'s hands in close-up doing the scene's real storytelling — twisting a wedding ring, sliding a key across a table, hesitating over a phone contact, folding and refolding a note. Shallow macro focus, lighting matched to the master's source. The cutaway every editor begs for: emotion located in the hands when the face is holding its secret.

title: Reaction Single
prompt: Clean single for reaction coverage: [SUBJECT] framed chest-up against gentle bokeh, listening to off-screen dialogue — the performance entirely in micro-shifts: a blink held too long, jaw tension, the breath before deciding not to speak. 75mm, eyeline close to lens, key matched to the scene's master. Hold longer than feels natural; the edit will thank you.

title: Rack Focus Two Truths
prompt: Static frame, two planes: [SUBJECT] sharp in the foreground saying one thing, while deep in the soft background a second figure does something that contradicts it. A deliberate rack focus trades the planes mid-line — the foreground melting, the background snapping into evidence. 85mm wide open. The cut that happens inside a single shot.

title: The Held Master
prompt: A single locked wide master that refuses to cut: the whole scene — entrance, argument, retreat, the slammed door, the long aftermath of one person alone in the frame — played out in real time at real distance. Furniture and doorways doing the blocking. 32mm, practical light only. Trust the geography and the performance; the audience edits with their eyes.

title: Mirror Without the Camera
prompt: A full dialogue scene played in a dressing-room mirror: both faces visible only as reflections, vanity bulbs framing them, the real bodies soft at frame's edge. Blocking choreographed so the camera never appears. When [SUBJECT] finally turns from the mirror to face the other directly, the shot cuts to reality for the first time — and the room feels colder there.

title: Slow Zoom Lie Detector
prompt: A near-imperceptible zoom during a single answer: [SUBJECT] explains where they were that night, calm and credible — while the lens creeps from medium to close over forty seconds, the tightening frame doing the interrogation no one in the room conducts. By the answer's end the eyes fill the frame, and one blink lands exactly where the truth would have a seam.

title: The Walk-Away Hold
prompt: After the scene's last line, [SUBJECT] exits — and the camera doesn't follow. The locked frame holds on [LOCATION: the emptied porch], the screen door settling, their footsteps fading, a wind chime taking over the soundtrack. The hold runs ten seconds past comfortable: absence given screen time, the room remembering them. Cut only when the audience has finished feeling it.

title: Kitchen Argument Two-Shot
prompt: Static two-shot across a kitchen island at night: [SUBJECT A] says quietly, "You knew. The whole time, you knew." [SUBJECT B] keeps drying the same plate, doesn't look up, replies "It wasn't the whole time." Long silence held in the frame; their eyes meet once in the dark window's reflection and flick away. Warm under-cabinet practicals, the rest of the house dark behind them. 40mm, both faces in focus — no cutting, the distance between them is the shot. Refrigerator hum under the silence.

title: Interrogation Across the Table
prompt: A bare room, one overhead lamp over a steel table. The camera slowly tracks side to side behind the detective's shoulder as they ask, "Walk me through the hour you can't account for." [SUBJECT] leans into the light, holding the detective's stare two beats too long before answering, eyes flat and unhurried: "I already did. You just didn't like it." Smoke hangs in the lamp cone. Hard top-light carving both faces, walls in blackness. 50mm, tension carried in pauses, chair creak, fluorescent buzz beyond the door.

title: Whispered Confession in Church
prompt: Slow push toward two figures in an empty church pew, shot from the row behind. Colored light from stained glass pools across them. [SUBJECT] whispers without turning: "If I tell you, you can't un-know it." The other simply nods once. Voices barely above breath, the vast reverberant space swallowing the words. 75mm, heads framed against blurred candle banks. Echoing stillness, distant door closing somewhere.

title: Doorway Breakup
prompt: The breakup staged on a threshold: [SUBJECT A] inside the apartment, [SUBJECT B] in the hallway, the doorframe splitting the frame and the lighting — warm interior against cold corridor. "Just say it here, then." — said to the floor, eyes lifting only for the answer. The door's edge under one hand the whole scene, the architecture asking the question before anyone answers it. 40mm static. The close is a door, slow, no slam.

title: Shot/Reverse: Matching Close-Ups
prompt: The intimacy workhorse: matching close-up coverage, 65mm, eyelines tight to the lens on both sides so the audience sits inside the conversation. [SUBJECT A]'s eyes searching [SUBJECT B]'s face line by line; [SUBJECT B]'s reverse catching the exact moment a defense fails — a blink rate change, the mouth's small surrender before the words agree. Cut on the looks, not the lines. Backgrounds melted to nothing; only faces remain.

title: Dirty Singles: The Negotiation
prompt: Negotiation coverage in dirty singles — each close-up keeping the rival's blurred shoulder in frame so neither face is ever alone. [SUBJECT A] reads a number off a page and watches it land: [SUBJECT B]'s eyes hold steady but the pen stops moving — the tell. Counter-offer delivered with a small accommodating smile that the eyes decline to join. The pauses negotiated as hard as the figures. [LOCATION: Glass conference walls, city behind], everything reflective.

title: Waiting Room, 3 AM
prompt: Hospital waiting room two-shot, locked: [SUBJECT A] and [SUBJECT B] in plastic chairs under flat fluorescent, a vending machine humming color into the corner. Dialogue arrives in fragments with whole minutes between — eyes on the doors every time they swing, hope and dread doing the same face. One reaches over without looking and takes the other's hand; the conversation continues through the grip more than the words. The doors swing. Both stand.

title: Monologue Push-In
prompt: Slow dolly-in on [SUBJECT] seated against a window in soft gray side-light, delivering a confession directly to an unseen listener off-frame: "I tell people I don't remember it. That's the lie I'm best at." Their voice stays level but their hands betray them; their eyes hold the listener's until the hardest phrase, drop for it, and climb back up after. The push-in lands at a tight close-up exactly as the last line ends. 65mm, shallow focus, dust motes in the window light. No score — room tone only.

title: Silent Reaction Hold
prompt: Locked-off close-up of [SUBJECT] reading a short handwritten note. No dialogue. The performance is everything: a small exhale, a swallow, eyes returning to the top of the note to read it again, jaw setting. The shot holds well past comfortable. 85mm, soft single key with gentle falloff, [LOCATION: a neutral background]. Paper rustle and breath are the only sounds. Restraint over melodrama.

title: The Calm Explanation
prompt: A villain's monologue played quiet: [SUBJECT] seated in [LOCATION: a dim study], hands folded, explaining exactly what is going to happen and why — no raised voice, eyes warm and absolutely unblinking, the menace living in how reasonable it all sounds. A small genuine smile at their own logic. Once, only once, the eyes go cold mid-sentence and return to warm so fast the listener doubts they saw it. The push stops just inside too close.

title: Single-Candle Chiaroscuro
prompt: Locked-off medium close-up of [SUBJECT] writing a letter by a single candle in a dark stone room. The candle is the only light source — deep Rembrandt shadows, warm flickering highlights on skin and paper, the background falling to pure black. 85mm lens, very shallow focus. The flame trembles when they exhale. Shot like an 18th-century oil painting lit only by its own flame, painterly, quiet scratching of pen on paper.

title: Fluorescent Office Dread
prompt: Slow push down an empty office corridor at 3 AM, lit by flat green-tinted fluorescent tubes, one fixture strobing irregularly at the far end. Symmetrical one-point perspective, every door closed. The hum of the ballasts is the only sound until a phone starts ringing in a distant room. 32mm lens, clinical sterile grade, cold one-point-perspective symmetry. Mounting unease without showing anything.

title: Neon Rain Close-Up
prompt: Tight slow-orbiting close-up of [SUBJECT]'s face at night in heavy rain, lit entirely by an unseen animated neon sign — the colors cycling magenta, cyan, magenta across wet skin. Raindrops on eyelashes in razor-thin focus. 65mm lens, anamorphic oval bokeh of blurred signage behind. Rain hiss and the electric buzz of the sign. Saturated romantic neon melancholy, time slowed by longing.

title: Moonlight Bedroom
prompt: Night bedroom in believable moonlight: one soft blue-white source raking through the window, sculpting the bed in cool half-tones, everything else falling to readable darkness — not video-game blue, just cold and quiet. [SUBJECT] awake, eyes open to the ceiling. A passing car's headlights sweep the wall once and leave. 50mm slow push. Insomnia's official lighting plan.

title: Phone-Lit Face
prompt: Close shot of [SUBJECT] in [LOCATION: a dark room] lit solely by their phone — cold under-glow modeling the face from below, screen light shifting as they scroll, the message they stop on visible only as a change in their eyes and a brightness tick on their skin. The rest of the world fully black. 65mm. The contemporary candle: intimate, isolating, story-loaded.

title: Police Lights Through Curtains
prompt: Interior night: red and blue pulses wash through the curtains of [LOCATION: a quiet living room], sweeping the walls in alternation, no siren — just the silent lights painting the family photos in turns of alarm. [SUBJECT] at the window's edge, one finger parting the fabric, face strobing between the two colors and the dark, deciding whether tonight is about them.

title: Steam Room Shafts
prompt: Inside [LOCATION: a tiled bathhouse]: skylight shafts dropping through dense steam, bodies as soft silhouettes appearing and dissolving in the white, water sounds mapping a space the eye can't. The push drifts until [SUBJECT] resolves from the haze, seated, head back, a sculpture being rinsed back into existence. Light made physical; air you can grade.

title: Mirror Discipline
prompt: Locked bathroom shot framing [SUBJECT] and their reflection at the sink. They lean down to splash water — the camera holds on the mirror — and when they rise, everything is normal. They leave; the reflection lingers one extra beat before following. No sting, no zoom, nothing underlined. A single bare bulb, dripping tap. The scare planted quietly for the viewer who was paying attention.

title: Background Figure Focus
prompt: Static shot of [SUBJECT] in sharp foreground reading on the couch, [LOCATION: the dark hallway] behind them a soft blur. Slowly, the focus racks back: a pale figure stands at the hallway's end, perfectly still. Focus returns to [SUBJECT], unaware. When it racks back again, the hallway is empty. 85mm shallow depth as the weapon. Page turns, a clock, and the awful patience of the frame.

title: POV: Opening the Door
prompt: First-person POV coverage: a hand (the viewer's own) enters frame low, turns a door handle, and pushes — [LOCATION: the room beyond] revealed exactly as the character discovers it, the camera making the small human adjustments of a real head: a settle, a scan left-to-right, a held beat of hesitation on the threshold, then a fix on the one detail that matters — the gaze tightening like a breath caught. 28mm, natural sway. Discovery shot from inside the discovering.

title: Waking in the Ward
prompt: First-person waking: black, then a slow blink-open — ceiling tiles soft, doubling, resolving. The POV's focus hunts like real waking eyes: a hanging IV line, a window too bright to hold, then a face leaning in from the side that takes three blinks to become someone loved. Their relief breaks as the POV steadies on them. A hand squeezes — felt at frame's edge — and the eyes (the camera) go glassy, the world swimming once more, this time not from the drugs.

title: Canvas and Count
prompt: Boxer's POV taking the shot that drops you: the world snaps sideways — ring lights wheeling, canvas arriving huge, sound dropping into deep water. The referee's face leaning in upside-down, his count arriving on delay, fingers blurred to bunches. The POV's choice rendered visually: corner shouting in silence, the rope within reach, the gym at fourteen, your own breath the loudest thing alive. The frame steadies by force of will. The canvas pushes back through the gloves. Up at eight. Single continuous take unless a lens switch is written; characters stay consistent and singular; grounded, stable physics throughout.

title: Storm Manor
prompt: Establishing shot of an isolated gothic manor on a hill against a churning storm sky — bare trees thrashing along the drive, a single lit window in the east wing, iron gates swinging unlatched. A slow low push up the driveway as lightning silhouettes the rooflines and gargoyles. Rain starting in fat drops on the lens. The house introduced as a character with intentions.

title: The Lit Window
prompt: Night establishing shot of a quiet suburban street — identical dark houses, sprinkler mist drifting through a streetlamp cone — except one house with a single upstairs window lit. A slow push down the street toward that window, a silhouette crossing behind the curtain once. Cicadas, a dog barking two yards over, a car passing somewhere parallel. Suburbia asleep, except for whoever's story this is.

title: College Quad, October
prompt: Aerial drift establishing a university quad in October: brick halls framing a lawn crisscrossed by walkers on the diagonal paths, maples gone fully gold and shedding with each gust, a clocktower throwing a long shadow across frisbee players and study blankets, the carillon striking the hour mid-shot. Leaves chasing each other in spirals. Institutional permanence wearing its prettiest month. Bells, leaf rustle, a marching band practicing far off.

title: 360 One-Take Performance
prompt: Single-take music video: the camera orbits [ARTIST] performing in [LOCATION: an empty warehouse] while the world assembles around them in real time — crew rolling in lights that flare on as they pass, dancers stepping into formation mid-orbit, a band materializing piece by piece — until the final revolution reveals a full show in progress. 28mm continuous orbit synced to the track's build. The video constructs itself.

title: Strobe-Cut Performance
prompt: Performance video built on strobe logic: pitch black, then a single strobe flash freezes [ARTIST] mid-move against a colored seamless — each flash a new pose, new outfit, new background color, cut exactly on the kick drum. The blackness between flashes carries the bass. Crisp fashion-lighting in each frozen frame, motion implied entirely through the jumps. Relentless, graphic, kinetic.

title: Projector Skin Performance
prompt: Intimate performance against black: [ARTIST] sung in close-up while projected imagery plays directly across their face and shoulders — home-video memories, rushing clouds, handwritten lyrics scrolling — the projection the only light source, conforming to every contour. The camera pushes slowly through the beam, catching dust drifting in the projector cone. Confessional verses, the imagery turning to static at the bridge.

title: Single Spotlight Ballad
prompt: Ballad staging at its most stripped: [ARTIST] on a stool in a void of black, one overhead spotlight, one microphone, dust drifting through the cone. The camera's slow orbit is the only motion for the whole first verse. At the bridge, the spot dims almost to nothing — the silhouette carries the climax — and blooms back for the final line. Voice, light, restraint.

title: Swinging Bulb
prompt: Performance lit by one swinging bulb in [LOCATION: a bare concrete room]: [ARTIST] stays planted while the bulb's arc drags light and shadow across them in rhythm — face appearing and vanishing on the downbeats, eyes catching the filament at each pass. The swing decays through the verse and is shoved back into motion for the chorus. Choreography for a light fixture; the performer as the constant.

title: Invisible Cut Through a Window
prompt: The camera pushes across a courtyard toward a lit apartment window at dusk, and passes seamlessly through the glass without a visible cut, continuing into the room where a family is setting a table for dinner — conversation mid-flow, as if we were always inside. Exterior cool blue gives way to warm interior tungsten as the camera crosses the threshold. Muffled street sound swells into room audio at the pass-through.

title: Dream Logic Dissolve Chain
prompt: A floating, slightly slow camera drifts through a chain of dissolves with dream logic: a hallway of fluttering curtains dissolves into a forest where the same curtains hang from the trees, which dissolves into a flooded ballroom where the trees stand in shallow mirror-water. [SUBJECT] appears in each space, always a little farther away. Soft diffusion, long dissolves that overlap movement, sound smearing between spaces like a memory.
